methodology: DayDine RCS v3.4.1 — V4 official-source mode with deterministic tie-breaks

headline.tracking_status: Strong public visibility; protect position and monitor nearby score clusters.

components.name: Trust & Compliance
components.summary: Compliance evidence from FHRS/FSA. This is a trust signal, not a food-quality score.

components.name: Customer Validation
components.summary: Single-platform evidence reduces confidence breadth even when rating strength is good.

components.name: Commercial Readiness
components.summary: Measures whether a guest can find, understand and act on the venue online.

monthly_tracking.label: Monthly movement
monthly_tracking.meaning: Future snapshots can show up/down movement

priorities.title: Add or strengthen a second legitimate customer-platform listing
priorities.why: The dashboard currently sees only one customer-platform family, which weakens confidence breadth.
priorities.action: Claim, clean or strengthen a suitable second listing such as TripAdvisor, OpenTable or another legitimate booking/review platform where appropriate.
priorities.expected_upside: Improves evidence breadth and may strengthen confidence class over time.

priorities.title: Protect rank in the local score cluster
priorities.why: The venue is currently ranked #7 and nearby venues may be separated by small score gaps.
priorities.action: Monitor review-count movement, listing accuracy and any inspection changes monthly.
priorities.expected_upside: Protects public visibility and gives early warning of competitor movement.

commercial_impact.wording_guardrail: Directional estimate only. Exact numbers require internal cover and spend data.

watch_list: Emergence or loss of a second review/listing platform

movement.summary: Baseline month — movement will appear after the next monthly snapshot.
